Details: One paper demonstrated that chromium (VI) can be co-precipitated (up to 75 percent) in presence of other heavy metals such as copper (II), nickel (II), and zinc (II). However, chromium (VI) removal in the presence of only nickel or zinc is negligible (5 to 8 percent). Another study found the use of a reducing agent, such as stannous chloride, sulfide, and sulfite can reduce chromium (VI) by up to 80% at pH 5.
